key_insight: A fully discrete implicit θ-scheme combined with WG-FEM on a Shishkin mesh yields parameter-uniform stability and error control for parabolic singularly perturbed boundary turning point problems.
engineering_relevance: The method provides a robust numerical tool for stiff transient PDEs with sharp boundary/interior layers, helping engineers obtain reliable simulations without losing accuracy as perturbation parameters become small.
method: Implicit θ-scheme for time discretization on a uniform temporal mesh
Weak Galerkin FEM for spatial discretization on a layer-adapted Shishkin mesh
Energy-norm error analysis with uniform convergence proofs
evidence_signal: The abstract reports rigorous stability and error estimates for both semi-discrete and fully discrete cases, and numerical tests confirm the theoretical convergence behavior.
watch_item: Implementation quality may depend on careful Shishkin mesh design and parameter choice, since layer resolution is critical for preserving uniform accuracy.
paper_title: Error Analysis of a Fully-Discrete Implicit $θ$-Scheme with WG-FEM for Parabolic Singularly Perturbed Boundary Turning Point Problems
